greeting: Hi, I'm Clair — your data-science analyst. Load a dataset or ask me to analyze one, and I'll take it from there.

artifact.title: Data Summary
artifact.body:
# Titanic Dataset Summary

**Shape**: 891 rows × 12 columns

## Columns
| Column | Type | Missing Values |
|--------|------|----------------|
| PassengerId | BIGINT | 0 |
| Survived | BIGINT | 0 |
| Pclass | BIGINT | 0 |
| Name | VARCHAR | 0 |
| Sex | VARCHAR | 0 |
| Age | DOUBLE | 177 |
| SibSp | BIGINT | 0 |
| Parch | BIGINT | 0 |
| Ticket | VARCHAR | 0 |
| Fare | DOUBLE | 0 |
| Cabin | VARCHAR | 687 |
| Embarked | VARCHAR | 2 |

## Data‑quality observations
- **Missing values**: Age (≈20% of rows), Cabin (≈77% of rows), Embarked (≈0.2%).
- **Unique identifier**: `PassengerId` is unique for all rows.
- No duplicate rows detected.
- All other fields are fully populated.

The dataset is typical for the classic Titanic survival prediction challenge and can be used for classification modeling after handling the missing values (e.g., imputing Age, dropping Cabin, and filling Embarked).

call.title: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_rows,
call.code:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_rows,
       SUM(CASE WHEN PassengerId IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS missing_PassengerId,
       SUM(CASE WHEN Survived IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS missing_Survived,
       SUM(CASE WHEN Pclass IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS missing_Pclass,
       SUM(CASE WHEN Name IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS missing_Name,
       SUM(CASE WHEN Sex IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS missing_Sex,
       SUM(CASE WHEN Age IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS missing_Age,
       SUM(CASE WHEN SibSp IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS missing_SibSp,
       SUM(CASE WHEN Parch IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS missing_Parch,
       SUM(CASE WHEN Ticket IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS missing_Ticket,
       SUM(CASE WHEN Fare IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS missing_Fare,
       SUM(CASE WHEN Cabin IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS missing_Cabin,
       SUM(CASE WHEN Embarked IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS missing_Embarked
FROM titanic
call.output: Failed to execute command 'execute': Unsupported JDBCType: OTHER

call.title: SELECT COUNT(*) - COUNT(PassengerId) AS missing_PassengerId,
call.code:
SELECT COUNT(*) - COUNT(PassengerId) AS missing_PassengerId,
       COUNT(*) - COUNT(Survived) AS missing_Survived,
       COUNT(*) - COUNT(Pclass) AS missing_Pclass,
       COUNT(*) - COUNT(Name) AS missing_Name,
       COUNT(*) - COUNT(Sex) AS missing_Sex,
       COUNT(*) - COUNT(Age) AS missing_Age,
       COUNT(*) - COUNT(SibSp) AS missing_SibSp,
       COUNT(*) - COUNT(Parch) AS missing_Parch,
       COUNT(*) - COUNT(Ticket) AS missing_Ticket,
       COUNT(*) - COUNT(Fare) AS missing_Fare,
       COUNT(*) - COUNT(Cabin) AS missing_Cabin,
       COUNT(*) - COUNT(Embarked) AS missing_Embarked
FROM titanic

**Shape**: 891 rows × 12 columns

## Columns and Data Types

|--------|------|----------|
| PassengerId | BIGINT | YES |
| Survived | BIGINT | YES |

| Name | VARCHAR | YES |

| Parch | BIGINT | YES |
| Ticket | VARCHAR | YES |
| Fare | DOUBLE | YES |

## Missing Values
| Column | Missing Count |
|--------|---------------|
| Age | 177 |
| Cabin | 687 |
| Embarked | 2 |
| (All other columns) | 0 |

### Notes
- The dataset contains a substantial number of missing values for **Age** and **Cabin**, which may need imputation or exclusion depending on the analysis.
- Only two records are missing the **Embarked** port information.

These observations should guide any preprocessing steps before modeling.

Dataset Summary

**Shape**: 891 rows × 12 columns

## Columns and Data Types

| Column | Type | Nullable |
|--------|------|----------|
| PassengerId | BIGINT | YES |
| Surv

ived | BIGINT | YES |
| Pclass | BIGINT | YES |
| Name | VARCHAR | YES |
| Sex | VARCHAR | YES |
| Age

| DOUBLE | YES |
| SibSp | BIGINT | YES |
| Parch | BIGINT | YES |
| Ticket

| VARCHAR | YES |
| Fare | DOUBLE | YES |
| Cabin | VARCHAR | YES |
| Emb

arked | VARCHAR | YES |

## Missing Values
| Column | Missing Count |
|--------|---------------|
| Age | 177 |
| Cabin |
**Cabin** have many missing values; consider imputation or removal for modeling.

- Only two rows lack **Embarked** information.

These insights
should guide preprocessing before any analysis or modeling.
